channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Supreme Court says U.S. can turn away asylum-seekers at the border
item.description: By a 6 to 3 vote, the high court ruled that that federal law allows the government to to stop asylum-seekers from physically setting foot in the United States, effectively keeping them from applying for asylum.
item.content:encoded:
By a 6 to 3 vote, the high court ruled that that federal law allows the government to to stop asylum-seekers from physically setting foot in the United States, effectively keeping them from applying for asylum. 

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Trump can begin deportations of Syrian, Haitian TPS holders, Supreme Court says
item.description: Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.
item.content:encoded:
Writing for the court majority, Justice Samuel Alito that under the TPS law, the president has unreviewable authority to end the program, without intervention from the courts.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: The Viking chant that became Norway's World Cup rallying cry
item.description: Norway's long-awaited World Cup return is being powered by a viral Viking "rowing" chant that's sweeping the world, from Boston train stations to Times Square — and the heart of Norway's parliament.
item.content:encoded:
Norway's long-awaited World Cup return is being powered by a viral Viking "rowing" chant that's sweeping the world, from Boston train stations to Times Square — and the heart of Norway's parliament.

(Image credit: Justin Setterfield)
item.dc:creator: William Jones

item.title: Supreme Court bars 'vampire rules' on gun ownership
item.description: In a 6-3 ideologically divided decision, the high court said that requiring permission in advance is an undue burden on the right to possess and carry a firearm.
item.content:encoded:
In a 6-3 ideologically divided decision, the high court said that requiring permission in advance is an undue burden on the right to possess and carry a firearm.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court backs Monsanto in its fight against liability from popular weed killer
item.description: The central issue in the Roundup case, filed by Missouri resident John Durnell, was who decides what should appear on a pesticide or insecticide label—and whether a federal law overrides state claims.
item.content:encoded:
The central issue in the Roundup case, filed by Missouri resident John Durnell, was who decides what should appear on a pesticide or insecticide label—and whether a federal law overrides state claims.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of Trump's order to limit voting by mail
item.description: A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of President Trump's executive order to limit voting by mail. The Trump administration is expected to appeal the ruling.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge in Boston has blocked parts of President Trump's executive order to limit voting by mail. The Trump administration is expected to appeal the ruling.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
item.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

(Image credit: Manaure Quintero)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 4 surprising things to know about abortion in America since Dobbs
item.description: A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.
item.content:encoded:
. '/>

A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: 'They can kill you': Immigrants fear a surge in xenophobic violence in South Africa
item.description: Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.
item.content:encoded:
Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.

(Image credit: Themba Hadebe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Long before the World Cup, Ukrainian immigrants built a soccer powerhouse in Philly
item.description: World Cup games are underway in Philadelphia. Long before Americans caught the world's soccer craze, Ukrainian migrants made Philly a soccer town. Today, the sport helps sustain their culture.
item.content:encoded:
World Cup games are underway in Philadelphia. Long before Americans caught the world's soccer craze, Ukrainian migrants made Philly a soccer town. Today, the sport helps sustain their culture.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
